name: Kano Model Feature Classification Ontology
alternateName: KANO-ONT — Kano Survey, Classification, Satisfaction Curves, Decay Tracking & Feature Prioritisation
description: Classifies product/service features by customer satisfaction response using the Kano Model (Noriaki Kano, 1984). Provides structured survey instruments, statistical classification of features into Kano categories (Must-Be, Performance, Attractive, Indifferent, Reverse), non-linear satisfaction curve modelling, temporal decay tracking (category migration over time), and aggregated feature prioritisation output. Operates as a PARALLEL ANALYTICAL LENS enriching the VP-to-PMF bridge with feature-level satisfaction attribution. Part of VE-Series VSOM-SA (Strategy Analysis).
oaa:domain: Customer Satisfaction Analysis, Feature Classification, Product Strategy

oaa:joinPatterns.name: VP Benefit Kano Classification
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Value proposition benefits are classified by Kano analysis to determine their satisfaction impact category, enabling feature-level investment decisions.
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Classify each VP benefit as Must-Be/Performance/Attractive to guide product roadmap priorities

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Kano Classification Informs PMF Validation
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Kano classification results inform PMF validation signals, providing satisfaction-attribution evidence for product-market fit assessment.
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Kano survey evidence feeds PMF validation to distinguish real fit from feature addiction

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Satisfaction Curve KPI Measurement
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Kano satisfaction curves are measured by KPIs to track implementation-level vs satisfaction-level correlation over time.
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Map non-linear satisfaction functions to measurable KPIs for continuous monitoring

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Kano Decay Triggers Pivot Assessment
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Category decay (e.g. Attractive degrades to Must-Be) triggers a PMF pivot assessment, signalling that the competitive landscape has shifted.
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Detect feature commoditisation early and trigger strategic pivot evaluation

oaa:joinPatterns.name: MECE Tree Assigns Kano Framework
oaa:joinPatterns.description: REASON-ONT MECE decomposition trees can assign the Kano framework as an analytical lens for feature-level problem structuring.
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Use MECE decomposition to systematically assign Kano categories to feature sets

oaa:enums.name: KanoCategory
oaa:enums.description: The five Kano Model feature classification categories (Noriaki Kano, 1984)

values.description: Basic expectations — absence causes extreme dissatisfaction, presence does not increase satisfaction (asymptotic negative curve)
Linear satisfiers — satisfaction scales proportionally with implementation level (linear curve)
Delighters — absence does not cause dissatisfaction, but presence creates disproportionate satisfaction (exponential curve)
Features that do not significantly affect satisfaction regardless of implementation (flat curve)
Features where implementation actively decreases satisfaction for some segments (inverse curve)

oaa:enums.name: SurveyStatus
oaa:enums.description: Lifecycle status of a Kano survey instrument

values.description: Survey designed but not yet deployed
Survey currently collecting responses
Survey data collection finished, results available

oaa:enums.name: CurveFunction
oaa:enums.description: Mathematical function type for satisfaction curve modelling

values.description: Must-Be: approaches zero satisfaction rapidly when absent, flat when present
Performance: proportional satisfaction-implementation relationship
Attractive: exponential satisfaction growth above baseline
Indifferent: minimal satisfaction variance regardless of implementation
Reverse: negative satisfaction correlation with implementation

oaa:enums.name: EvidenceStrength
oaa:enums.description: Statistical strength of the Kano classification evidence

values.description: High sample size, high confidence, clear category dominance
Adequate sample, moderate confidence, category leads but not dominant
Low sample or ambiguous results, classification provisional

oaa:enums.name: CompetitivePressure
oaa:enums.description: Level of competitive pressure driving category decay

values.description: Multiple competitors have commoditised this feature
Some competitors offer equivalent — decay in progress
Feature still relatively differentiated

oaa:enums.name: DecayAction
oaa:enums.description: Recommended strategic action in response to category decay

values.description: Create next-generation Attractive replacement
Speed up delivery before further commoditisation
Accept category migration, maintain as hygiene factor
Track decay trajectory, no immediate action required

oaa:enums.name: InvestmentRecommendation
oaa:enums.description: Investment recommendation based on Kano analysis output

values.description: Attractive or high-impact Performance — increase investment
Must-Be or stable Performance — maintain current investment
Indifferent — reduce investment, reallocate to higher-impact features
Reverse — remove or redesign, feature actively harms satisfaction

entities.name: KanoSurvey
entities.description: Complete Kano survey instrument targeting a specific customer segment. Contains functional/dysfunctional question pairs for each feature under evaluation. Links to vp:IdealCustomerProfile and optionally pmf:CustomerSegmentFit for segment-specific analysis.

oaa:properties.name: surveyId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for this Kano survey

oaa:properties.name: name
oaa:properties.description: Descriptive name for this survey

oaa:properties.name: targetICPRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vp:IdealCustomerProfile @id — the target customer segment

oaa:properties.name: surveyDate
oaa:properties.description: Date when the survey was conducted or started

oaa:properties.name: sampleSize
oaa:properties.description: Number of respondents in this survey

oaa:properties.name: confidenceLevel
oaa:properties.description: Statistical confidence level (0.0-1.0, e.g. 0.95 for 95%)

oaa:properties.name: segmentRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to pmf:CustomerSegmentFit @id for segment-specific analysis

oaa:properties.name: status
oaa:properties.description: Current lifecycle status of this survey

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: Narrative description of survey scope and objectives

entities.name: KanoQuestion
entities.description: Functional/dysfunctional question pair for a single feature under Kano evaluation. The functional question asks about satisfaction when the feature IS present; the dysfunctional question asks about satisfaction when the feature is NOT present. Responses are cross-tabulated to determine the Kano category.

oaa:properties.name: questionId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for this question pair

oaa:properties.name: featureRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vp:Benefit or vp:Solution @id — the feature being evaluated

oaa:properties.name: functionalQuestion
oaa:properties.description: The functional form: How would you feel IF this feature is present?

oaa:properties.name: dysfunctionalQuestion
oaa:properties.description: The dysfunctional form: How would you feel IF this feature is NOT present?

oaa:properties.name: surveyRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to kano:KanoSurvey @id this question belongs to

oaa:properties.name: responseScale
oaa:properties.description: Response scale type — standard five-point Kano scale (Like, Expect, Neutral, Live-With, Dislike)

oaa:properties.name: featureDescription
oaa:properties.description: Plain-language description of the feature for respondents

entities.name: KanoClassification
entities.description: Feature-to-category assignment resulting from Kano survey cross-tabulation. Each classification assigns a Kano category (Must-Be, Performance, Attractive, Indifferent, Reverse) with statistical confidence. The same feature CAN have different categories across different customer segments (BR-KANO-007).

oaa:properties.name: classificationId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for this classification result

oaa:properties.name: featureRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vp:Benefit or vp:Solution @id being classified

oaa:properties.name: category
oaa:properties.description: Assigned Kano category from cross-tabulation analysis

oaa:properties.name: confidence
oaa:properties.description: Statistical confidence of this classification (0.0-1.0). MUST be >=0.6 for production use (BR-KANO-003)

oaa:properties.name: sampleSize
oaa:properties.description: Number of respondents contributing to this classification

oaa:properties.name: classificationDate
oaa:properties.description: Date when classification was computed

oaa:properties.name: surveyRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to kano:KanoSurvey @id providing the source data

oaa:properties.name: segmentRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to pmf:CustomerSegmentFit @id — enables segment-level classification

oaa:properties.name: evidenceStrength
oaa:properties.description: Qualitative assessment of evidence strength

oaa:properties.name: categoryDistribution
oaa:properties.description: JSON-encoded distribution across all five categories (e.g. {MustBe:0.45, Performance:0.30, ...})

entities.name: SatisfactionCurve
entities.description: Non-linear satisfaction function model per Kano category. Each category has a characteristic curve shape: Must-Be (asymptotic), Performance (linear), Attractive (exponential), Indifferent (flat), Reverse (inverse). Used for investment modelling — predicts satisfaction return on implementation investment.

oaa:properties.name: curveId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for this satisfaction curve

oaa:properties.name: name
oaa:properties.description: Descriptive name for this curve model

oaa:properties.name: categoryType
oaa:properties.description: Kano category this curve models

oaa:properties.name: curveFunction
oaa:properties.description: Mathematical function type of the satisfaction curve

oaa:properties.name: inflectionPoints
oaa:properties.description: Implementation levels where satisfaction response changes character

oaa:properties.name: customerSegment
oaa:properties.description: Customer segment this curve applies to (may vary by segment)

oaa:properties.name: implementationLevel
oaa:properties.description: Current implementation level (0-100 scale)

oaa:properties.name: satisfactionLevel
oaa:properties.description: Current satisfaction level (0-100 scale)

oaa:properties.name: kpiRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to kpi:KPI @id measuring this satisfaction dimension

entities.name: KanoDecay
entities.description: Tracks temporal category migration (decay) of features over time. The classic Kano decay pattern is Attractive -> Performance -> Must-Be as competitors commoditise features. Decay detection triggers strategic responses (Innovate, Accelerate, Accept, Monitor) and can trigger PMF pivot assessments.

oaa:properties.name: decayId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for this decay observation

oaa:properties.name: featureRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vp:Benefit or vp:Solution @id experiencing decay

oaa:properties.name: fromCategory
oaa:properties.description: Original Kano category before decay

oaa:properties.name: toCategory
oaa:properties.description: New Kano category after decay. MUST NOT equal fromCategory (BR-KANO-004)

oaa:properties.name: decayPeriodMonths
oaa:properties.description: Number of months over which decay was observed

oaa:properties.name: detectionDate
oaa:properties.description: Date when the category migration was detected

oaa:properties.name: evidence
oaa:properties.description: Evidence supporting the decay detection (e.g. repeat survey results, competitor analysis)

oaa:properties.name: competitivePressure
oaa:properties.description: Level of competitive pressure driving the decay

oaa:properties.name: recommendedAction
oaa:properties.description: Recommended strategic action in response to the decay

oaa:properties.name: pivotAssessmentRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to pmf:PivotAssessment @id if decay triggers pivot evaluation

entities.name: FeaturePriority
entities.description: Aggregated feature prioritisation output derived from Kano classification analysis. Combines category assignment, confidence, segment variation, and willingness-to-pay elasticity into an actionable investment recommendation. Serves as the primary decision-support output of the Kano analytical lens.

oaa:properties.name: priorityId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for this priority assessment

oaa:properties.name: name
oaa:properties.description: Feature name or descriptive label

oaa:properties.name: featureRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vp:Benefit or vp:Solution @id being prioritised

oaa:properties.name: priorityRank
oaa:properties.description: Ordinal priority rank (1 = highest priority)

oaa:properties.name: investmentRecommendation
oaa:properties.description: Strategic investment recommendation based on Kano evidence

oaa:properties.name: kanoEvidenceRefs
oaa:properties.description: References to kano:KanoClassification @ids providing evidence for this priority

oaa:properties.name: wtpElasticity
oaa:properties.description: Willingness-to-pay elasticity (0.0-2.0): how much customers will pay more for this feature. >1.0 = elastic, <1.0 = inelastic

oaa:properties.name: hasSegmentVariation
oaa:properties.description: Whether this feature has different Kano categories across customer segments

oaa:properties.name: strategicAction
oaa:properties.description: Narrative strategic action recommendation

oaa:properties.name: pmfWTPRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to pmf:WillingnessToPayAssessment @id for pricing linkage

relationships.name: Has Survey Question
relationships.description: A Kano survey contains functional/dysfunctional question pairs

relationships.name: Produces Classification
relationships.description: A Kano survey produces feature classifications through cross-tabulation analysis

relationships.name: Question Classifies As
relationships.description: A Kano question pair produces a classification result for its target feature

relationships.name: Classification Models Curve
relationships.description: A Kano classification implies a satisfaction curve model for the feature

relationships.name: Classification Decays To
relationships.description: A Kano classification may decay to a different category over time

relationships.name: Classification Informs Priority
relationships.description: One or more Kano classifications inform a feature priority recommendation

relationships.name: Decay Informs Priority
relationships.description: Decay observations may adjust feature priority recommendations

relationships.name: Classified By Kano
relationships.description: A VP benefit or solution is classified by a Kano analysis result

relationships.name: Survey Targets ICP
relationships.description: A Kano survey targets a specific ideal customer profile for segment-focused analysis

relationships.name: Survey Segment Fit
relationships.description: A Kano survey optionally links to a PMF customer segment fit for deeper segmentation

relationships.name: Informs Validation
relationships.description: A Kano classification informs a PMF validation signal with satisfaction-attribution evidence

relationships.name: Triggers Assessment
relationships.description: A Kano decay observation triggers a PMF pivot assessment when feature commoditisation is detected

relationships.name: Measured By KPI
relationships.description: A satisfaction curve is measured by one or more KPIs for continuous monitoring

relationships.name: Assigned Framework
relationships.description: A MECE decomposition tree assigns the Kano framework for feature-level analysis

relationships.name: Priority Links WTP
relationships.description: A feature priority links to a PMF willingness-to-pay assessment for pricing decision support

businessRules.name: Classification Must Reference Valid Feature
businessRules.action: THEN featureRef MUST reference a valid vp:Benefit or vp:Solution @id

businessRules.name: Survey Must Target ICP
businessRules.action: THEN targetICPRef MUST reference a valid vp:IdealCustomerProfile @id

businessRules.name: Production Classification Confidence Threshold
businessRules.condition: IF a KanoClassification is used for production investment decisions

businessRules.name: Decay Categories Must Differ
businessRules.action: THEN fromCategory MUST NOT equal toCategory

businessRules.name: Investment Recommendation Category Alignment
businessRules.action: THEN it SHOULD align with KanoCategory conventions: MustBe/Performance -> Invest/Maintain, Attractive -> Invest, Indifferent -> Deprioritise, Reverse -> Eliminate

businessRules.name: Survey Sample Size Minimum
businessRules.action: THEN sampleSize SHOULD be >= 30 per segment for statistical validity

businessRules.name: Cross-Segment Category Variation Permitted
businessRules.condition: IF the same feature has KanoClassification instances across different segments
businessRules.action: THEN different categories CAN be assigned — segment variation is expected and valid. Set FeaturePriority.hasSegmentVariation = true.

competencyQuestions.question: What is the Kano category classification for a given product feature, and with what statistical confidence was it assigned?
Which customer segment was surveyed, how many respondents participated, and what was the survey completion status?
What functional and dysfunctional question pair was used to evaluate a specific feature in the Kano survey?
What is the non-linear satisfaction function shape for a given Kano category, and what are its inflection points?
Has a feature experienced Kano category decay (e.g. Attractive -> Performance -> Must-Be), and what competitive pressure drove the migration?
What is the investment recommendation for a feature based on its Kano classification evidence, and does it vary across customer segments?
Which features have different Kano categories across customer segments, and what does this imply for segment-specific product strategy?
How does Kano decay evidence flow through to PMF pivot assessment, and when should a strategic pivot be triggered?

changeControl.changeNotes: Initial release of KANO-ONT as part of VE-Series VSOM-SA (Strategy Analysis). Provides Kano Model feature classification: survey instruments, functional/dysfunctional question pairs, statistical cross-tabulation classification, non-linear satisfaction curve modelling, temporal decay tracking, and aggregated feature prioritisation. Cross-references VP-ONT v1.2.3, PMF-ONT v2.0.0, KPI-ONT v1.0.0, REASON-ONT v1.0.0, PE-ONT v4.0.0.
